entries.name: Playwright Browser
entries.description: Browser automation through the official Playwright MCP server.
entries.readmeMarkdown:
## Playwright Browser

Drives a real browser over stdio: navigation, clicks, form fill and accessibility snapshots. Maintained by Microsoft.

entries.name: Browser Use
entries.description: Local agentic browser control via the browser-use CLI MCP server.
entries.readmeMarkdown:
## Browser Use

Runs the browser-use CLI as an MCP server for LLM-driven browsing. Requires `uv`/`uvx` and an `OPENAI_API_KEY`.

entries.name: Chrome DevTools
entries.description: Official Chrome DevTools MCP server for browser debugging, console inspection and performance trace analysis.
entries.readmeMarkdown:
## Chrome DevTools

Runs Google's Chrome DevTools MCP server over stdio. It can inspect pages, console output, network activity and performance trace data from a Chrome browser. Browser contents may be exposed to the agent, so avoid using it with sensitive sessions unless you explicitly trust the agent.

entries.name: GitHub
entries.description: Remote GitHub MCP server for repositories, issues and pull requests.
entries.readmeMarkdown:
## GitHub (remote)

The official remote GitHub MCP server. Supports OAuth or a Personal Access Token header. Phase 1 shows the connection details only.

entries.name: Figma
entries.description: Official remote Figma MCP server for design context, screenshots, variables and canvas workflows.
entries.readmeMarkdown:
## Figma Remote

Connects to Figma's official hosted MCP endpoint over Streamable HTTP. Authentication uses Figma OAuth with the `mcp:connect` scope. The remote server is link-based: copy a Figma frame, layer, FigJam, Slides or Make URL and use it in the agent prompt.

entries.name: Sentry
entries.description: Official hosted Sentry MCP service for issues, traces, projects and debugging workflows.
entries.readmeMarkdown:
## Sentry Remote

Connects to Sentry's hosted MCP service over Streamable HTTP with OAuth. It is focused on human-in-the-loop coding and debugging workflows such as issue, event and trace investigation.

entries.name: Vercel
entries.description: Official Vercel remote MCP server for docs, projects, deployments and logs.
entries.readmeMarkdown:
## Vercel Remote

Connects to Vercel's official remote MCP endpoint over Streamable HTTP with OAuth. Vercel documents this endpoint for project, deployment, log and documentation workflows.

entries.name: Grafana
entries.description: Official Grafana MCP server for dashboards, datasources, alerts and observability queries.
entries.readmeMarkdown:
## Grafana

Runs Grafana Labs' official `mcp-grafana` server over stdio. Provide `GRAFANA_URL` and a `GRAFANA_SERVICE_ACCOUNT_TOKEN`; Grafana Cloud instances should use their HTTPS instance URL.

entries.name: Netdata Cloud
entries.description: Official Netdata Cloud MCP endpoint for infrastructure metrics, nodes, alerts and functions.
prerequisites: Netdata Cloud API token with scope:mcp
entries.readmeMarkdown:
## Netdata Cloud

Connects directly to Netdata Cloud's HTTPS MCP endpoint over Streamable HTTP. Provide a Netdata Cloud API token with `scope:mcp`. Local Netdata Agent HTTP endpoints should be added through Custom MCP or an approved external registry entry.

entries.name: Notion
entries.description: Hosted remote MCP for Notion pages, databases and search.
entries.readmeMarkdown:
## Notion (hosted)

Notion's hosted MCP endpoint. Connection is via OAuth in the Phase 2 setup flow; Phase 1 only previews the endpoint.

entries.name: Filesystem
entries.description: Read and write files within an allowed workspace directory.
entries.readmeMarkdown:
## Filesystem

Reference MCP server for scoped file access. The `${WORKSPACE}` placeholder is filled with the chosen directory during setup.

entries.name: MarkItDown
entries.description: Convert documents, archives and rich files into Markdown through Microsoft's local MarkItDown MCP server.
entries.readmeMarkdown:
## MarkItDown

Runs Microsoft's local MarkItDown MCP server over STDIO for local trusted agents. It exposes `convert_to_markdown(uri)` for `file:`, `http:`, `https:`, and `data:` URIs. The server runs with the current user's file and network access, so review URI inputs and avoid exposing HTTP or SSE modes beyond localhost.

entries.name: Memory
entries.description: Persistent knowledge-graph memory via the reference MCP server.
entries.readmeMarkdown:
## Memory

Reference knowledge-graph memory server. Stores entities and relations the agent can recall across sessions.

entries.name: Slack Remote
entries.description: Official hosted Slack MCP server over Streamable HTTP.
entries.readmeMarkdown:
## Slack Remote

Connects to Slack's official hosted MCP endpoint. Requires a Slack app configured for OAuth.

entries.name: Fetch
entries.description: Fetch a URL and convert its contents to Markdown for the agent via the official local Fetch MCP server.
entries.readmeMarkdown:
## Fetch

Runs the official local `mcp-server-fetch` server over STDIO. It exposes a single `fetch(url)` tool that retrieves a web page and converts it to Markdown for the agent. Every fetch requires explicit confirmation.

This server can access local/internal IP addresses and may represent a security risk. Exercise caution when using this MCP server to ensure this does not expose any sensitive data.

entries.name: Discord
entries.description: Third-party Discord MCP server (IQAI) for messaging, channels, forums, reactions and webhooks via a Discord bot token.
prerequisites: Discord bot token from the Developer Portal
entries.readmeMarkdown:
## Discord (third-party, pending review)

Runs the **third-party** `@iqai/mcp-discord` server (IQAI) over stdio, pinned to `@0.0.6`. This is **not** an official Discord MCP server and is **pending PuPu review** (`needs_review`); the version is pinned so future releases are not auto-pulled without a re-vet. The bot token is read from the `DISCORD_TOKEN` environment variable, injected by the PuPu backend secret store — it is never written to localStorage.

### Least-privilege bot setup (required)

Before connecting, create a **dedicated Discord bot** for the agent and grant it the **minimum** permissions only:

- Do **not** grant the **Administrator** permission. Grant only the specific guild/channel permissions for the actions you want the agent to perform (e.g. Send Messages / Read Message History for the specific channels in use).
- **Scope the bot to a single guild (server)** wherever possible, rather than inviting it to multiple servers.
- When inviting the bot, select only the OAuth2 scopes/permissions it needs; avoid blanket permission integers.
- Treat the bot token as a secret: rotate it if it leaks, and remove the bot from servers you no longer want the agent to reach.

Every write, messaging, moderation, webhook and login tool requires explicit confirmation; only read-only tools (get server info, read messages, list/get forum posts) run without a prompt.

entries.name: Telegram
entries.description: Third-party Telegram MCP server (IQAI, Bot API / Telegraf) for sending, forwarding and pinning messages and reading channel info/members via a Telegram bot token.
entries.readmeMarkdown:
## Telegram (third-party, pending review)

Runs the **third-party** `@iqai/mcp-telegram` server (IQAI) over stdio, pinned to `@0.1.4`. This is built on the Telegram **Bot API** (Telegraf) and is **not** an official Telegram MCP server; it is **pending PuPu review** (`needs_review`). The version is pinned so future releases are not auto-pulled without a re-vet. The bot token is read from the `TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN` environment variable, injected by the PuPu backend secret store — it is never written to localStorage.

### Least-privilege bot setup (required)

Before connecting, create a **dedicated Telegram bot** for the agent and constrain its reach:

- Create the bot via [@BotFather](https://t.me/BotFather) and treat the token as a secret; rotate it if it leaks.
- Add the bot **only to the specific groups/channels** the agent needs. The bot's group/channel membership **is its blast radius** and is not enforceable in code — a bot can message, forward from, and pin in every chat it has joined.
- Enable **mention-only mode** (the package's `SAMPLING_MENTION_ONLY` is on by default) so the bot only reacts when explicitly mentioned in groups, rather than to all traffic.
- Remove the bot from any chat you no longer want the agent to reach.

### Forwarding is an exfiltration primitive

`FORWARD_MESSAGE` copies content from one chat into another and is gated behind explicit confirmation, alongside `SEND_MESSAGE` and `PIN_MESSAGE`. Only the read-only tools (get channel info, get channel members) run without a prompt.

entries.name: SQLite
entries.description: Query and manage a local SQLite database file through the SQLite reference MCP server.
entries.readmeMarkdown:
## SQLite (community / archived reference server)

Runs the official **`mcp-server-sqlite`** reference server over stdio via `uvx`, pinned to `==2025.4.25`. This server lives in the **archived** [`modelcontextprotocol/servers-archived`](https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/servers-archived/tree/main/src/sqlite) repository — it is a community/archived reference server, so the version is pinned and future releases are not auto-pulled without a re-vet. No connection string, password, or secret is required: the server only opens a **local SQLite database file** whose path is bound to your agent workspace (`--db-path ${WORKSPACE}`).

### Point it at a disposable, non-production database

The agent can read **every table and row** in the database file you connect — reading the database **is** data exfiltration. Connect a **disposable / non-production** `.db` file you are comfortable exposing in full; never point it at a production or sensitive database.

### Gated vs. ungated tools

The read tools — `read_query` (SELECT only), `list_tables`, and `describe_table` — run without a prompt. Every tool that **mutates state** is gated behind explicit confirmation:

- `write_query` — INSERT / UPDATE / DELETE.
- `create_table` — CREATE TABLE (DDL).
- `append_insight` — writes to the server's `memo://insights` resource.

Even with writes gated, treat the connected database as fully readable by the agent.
